person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Richard Fitun, alias Phitun, Fiton

Richard Phitun and John Phitun were among the witnesses at Macclesfield when Ranulf (III), earl of Chester, confirmed Marple and Wybersley to Robert, the son of Robert de Stokeport as his heritage. Barraclough dates the charter of this confirmation to 1206, however, Earwaker dates it to 1220.

Between 1225-1229 [dated by Barraclough], Ranulf III, earl of Chester and Lincoln, released to Vivian Davenport the rent of 3s which he rendered for Hysebelesbothes and the land of Peter the Smith in exchange for Hysebelesbothes, which Vivian quitclaimed to the earl, witnessed by: Philip de Orrebi the earl's justiciar at that time, William de Vernon, Richard Phiton, John son of Philip, Robert de Stokeport, Jordan de Bredburi, John Fiton, Henry de Wrthe, Thomas de Teteswrthe, Richard de Wibbenburi, and others.

Richard was the father Hugh Phitun.

At Lancaster, on the Octave of St Martin, 26 Henry III, [18th November, 1241], concerning the nativity of Alexander, son of William, and Adam, son of Aumund: Alexander and Adam paid 20s sterling to Richard Fiton for a quitclaim in which Richard, the plaintiff, acknowledged Alexander and Adam to be freemen and released them from all manner of nativity and servitude.

An inquisition during the reign of Henry III (date not recorded and writ missing), at Chester, regarding Richard Fitun, found Hugh Fitun, knight, was his heir, and Richard held the manor of Bolyn, 2 bovates land and a mill, with the advowson, held of the king in chief by service of coming to the army at Chester with all his household (cum tota familia sua) with horses and arms at the king's expense; and Falinisbrom, held of the king in chief by 3s rent. Fallibroome is a township in Prestbury parish, Cheshire, England.

Richard was dead before 4 June, 30 Henry III [1246], when the king, Henry III of England, at Windsor, having taken the homage of Hugh, son and heir of Richard Phitun, for all the lands and tenements which Richard held of the king in chief, ordered J. de Gray, justice of Chester that, having accepted security from the same Hugh for rendering 100s. to the king for his relief, he was to cause him to have seisin of all the lands and tenements of which Richard was seised as of fee on the day he died and which fall to him by hereditary right.

Disconnected Parents
This profile's original parents were John Fitton, born in 1260 and Cecilia Massey, born c1160. John is actually Richard's GG Grand Son, and another Cecilia Massey was married to John. Both of these have been disconnected from this profile.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight於大約1175年出生在英國爾法利布盧時，他的父親Sir Richard de Fitton27歲，母親Ciceley de Massey20歲。他於1194年與Ellen fitz Elias在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1246年在英國爾享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire):
from Middle English fiton, fittin ‘lie, falsehood’, for a lier or cheat.

possibly a habitational name from Fitton Hall in Leverington, Cambridgeshire, named in Anglo-Scandinavian as ‘settlement by the meadow-land’, from Old English tūn + Old Norse fit, a term denoting grassland on the bank of a river.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Bolyn,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Bullen  .
Flemish: derivative of the personal name Bol, from Baldwin .

variants.name: Bullen
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from the French Channel port of Boulogne, recorded in Latin sources both as Gessoriacum and as Bononia. The latter name is clearly the source of the modern placename. It is ostensibly a derivative of Latin bonus ‘good’ (compare Bolognese ), but may in fact come from a Gaulish element bona ‘foundation’. Boulogne has long been a major trading port between England and France.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight was born about 1175, in Fallibroome, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir Richard de Fitton, was 27 and his mother, Ciceley de Massey, was 20. He married Ellen fitz Elias in 1194, in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1246, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight est né environ 1175, à Fallibroome, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard de Fitton, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Ciceley de Massey, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Ellen fitz Elias en 1194, à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1246, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight um 1175 in Fallibroome, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard de Fitton 27 und seine Mutter Ciceley de Massey 20. Er hat 1194 in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England Ellen fitz Elias geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1246 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight è nato il circa 1175 nel Fallibroome, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard de Fitton, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Ciceley de Massey, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Ellen fitz Elias nel 1194, nel Wilmslow, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1246, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knightが約1175年にイギリスチェシュアイアファリブルーメで生まれた時，父Sir Richard de Fittonは27歳，母Ciceley de Masseyは20歳であった。1194年にイギリスチェシュアイアウィルムスロウでEllen fitz Eliasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1246年にイギリスチェシュアイアで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight(이)가 대략 1175년 영국 체실르 팰리브룸에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard de Fitton(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Ciceley de Massey(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1194년 영국 체실르 윌슬로에서 Ellen fitz Elias(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1246년 영국 체실르에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight nasceu aproximadamente 1175, em Fallibroome, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard de Fitton, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Ciceley de Massey, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen fitz Elias em 1194, em Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1246, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight родился около 1175 г. в местности фаллибрум, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard de Fitton было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Ciceley de Massey, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen fitz Elias в 1194 г. в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1246 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 72 лет.

sm.Between 1245 and 1246: mai le 1245 e o'o I le 1246

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Fitton - Lord of Bolyn, Knight nació aproximadamente 1175, en Fallibroome, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard de Fitton, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Ciceley de Massey, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Ellen fitz Elias en 1194, en Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1246, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años.
